push-up.description: From a high plank, lower chest toward the floor with elbows ~45° to torso, then press back to full extension without losing body line.

incline-push-up.description: Hands on a box or bench, body in a straight line. Lower chest toward the surface and press back to lockout without losing plank tension.
tips: Screw hands into surface to engage lats

archer-push-up.description: From a wide push-up stance, shift weight onto one arm while lowering toward it, keeping the other arm straight as a guide. Press back to center and alternate.
tips: Keep the straight arm active and pushing

body-row.description: Under a low bar or sturdy table, hold an overhand grip and keep your body straight like a plank as you pull your chest to the bar, then lower under control.
tips: Keep ribs down and glutes tight
Touch chest to bar each rep if possible

archer-bodyrow.description: In the body row position, perform rows while extending one arm to the side, shifting most of the pulling load to the bent arm.
tips: Pull with one arm while the other assists minimally
Control the extension on the way down

pull-up.description: From a dead hang, pull your body up until chin clears the bar, then lower with control back to full extension.

typewriter-pull-up.description: Pull up to the bar, then slide horizontally to one side until that arm is nearly straight, then slide to the other side before lowering. Keep chin above bar throughout.
tips: Pull to bar first, then initiate the slide
Keep core tight to avoid swinging

dip.description: On parallel bars, lower your body until shoulders dip below elbows, then press back to lockout with control.
tips: Slight forward lean for chest focus

pike-stand.description: From a downward-dog shape, walk your feet toward your hands to stack hips over shoulders. Hold the inverted V position while pressing the floor away.
tips: Push tall through shoulders; don't sink
Keep legs straight and heels lifted

active-hang.description: Hang from a pull-up bar with straight arms and engage shoulders by pulling them slightly down and back. Hold without bending elbows.
tips: Grip the bar evenly and breathe

skin-the-cat.description: Hang from a bar, tuck knees to chest and rotate backward through your arms until your body is inverted (German hang), then return the way you came.
tips: Move slowly—control each degree of rotation
Tuck first until you build shoulder mobility

hanging-side-lean.description: From an active hang, shift your hips to one side while keeping arms straight, creating a lateral body lean. Hold, then return to center and alternate sides.
tips: Initiate the lean from the hips, not by sagging

handstand-routine.description: A dedicated handstand practice block: kick up to a wall handstand, hold and work on alignment (ears between arms, hollow body, pointed toes), then rest and repeat.
tips: Maintain hollow-body tension from head to toe
Use fingertip pressure to fine-tune balance

l-sit.description: From a support on parallel bars or the floor, press down to lift hips and extend legs parallel to the ground. Hold the L-shaped position.
tips: Press firmly through palms to elevate hips
Tilt pelvis slightly posterior to flatten back
Lock knees and flex quads to keep legs up

rollout.description: From kneeling (or standing), grip an ab wheel or bar and roll forward until arms are fully extended overhead, then pull back to start without losing spinal control.
tips: Hollow body throughout—never let hips sag
Pull with lats and abs on the return
Start with a limited range until core is strong enough

planche-lean.description: In a push-up top position, shift shoulders forward past the wrists while keeping arms straight and body rigid in a hollow position. Hold the forward lean.
tips: Push the ground away to keep elbows locked

easy-bridge-lean.description: From a bridge position (hips lifted, feet and hands on floor), shift weight toward hands to load the shoulders. Hold the lean without losing hip height.
tips: Keep elbows pointed backward, not flared

dish-hold.description: Lie supine with arms extended overhead. Press lower back firmly into the floor and lift arms, head, and legs simultaneously to create a hollow dish shape. Hold.
tips: Lower back must stay glued to the floor
Arms and legs long and active

hollow-body-crunch.description: Lie supine, flatten lower back to the floor, lift shoulders and legs to create a hollow curve, then perform small controlled crunches without losing the hollow.
tips: Reach long through fingers and toes

hollow-body-hold.description: Lie supine, press lower back into floor, lift shoulders and legs creating a hollow banana shape. Hold the position.
tips: Lower back must stay pressed to floor
Arms extended overhead or by sides

sitting-leg-raise.description: Seated on the edge of a bench or box with hands supporting, lift both legs (knees bent or straight) toward the chest, then lower with control.
tips: Tilt pelvis slightly back to engage lower abs
Keep spine tall; avoid rounding into the lift

supporting-leg-raise.description: In a support hold on parallel bars (arms straight, body lifted), raise straight legs to horizontal or above, then lower with control.
tips: Lock elbows and keep shoulders depressed
Initiate with a posterior pelvic tilt

lunge.description: Step forward and lower your back knee toward the floor while keeping the front knee stacked over the ankle. Drive through the front foot to stand.
tips: Keep torso tall and hips square

jumping-lunge.description: Jump explosively from a lunge and switch legs mid-air, landing softly into the opposite lunge. Maintain torso control throughout.

jumping-squat.description: Squat down and explode upward into a jump, landing softly and immediately moving into the next rep with control.
tips: Load hips and keep chest proud

squat.description: With feet shoulder-width, sit the hips down and back while keeping chest lifted and knees tracking over toes. Stand to full extension.

pistol-squat.description: Stand on one leg with the other extended forward. Lower into a full single-leg squat until the hamstring touches the calf, then drive back to standing.
tips: Keep heel of working foot flat on floor

archer-squat.description: Stand wide, shift weight to one leg while squatting, keeping the other leg straight. Alternate sides.

step-up.description: Place one foot on a box/step. Drive through the whole foot to stand tall on the box, then lower with control.
tips: Lean slightly forward to load glute
Avoid pushing off the back leg

swimmer.description: Lying prone, lift opposite arm and leg alternately in a fluttering motion while keeping the trunk stable.
tips: Squeeze glutes to protect low back

arch-up.description: Lying prone, lift chest and legs off the floor by squeezing glutes and upper back, creating an arch through the spine.
tips: Reach long through fingers and toes
